MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DEAN A. PAPPAS, ESQ., 650.364.8200, FOR INTERVENOR EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY; No other appearanes.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff's suit seeks to recover damages based on International Window Corporation's defective manufacturing and misrepresentations. Plaintiff does not seek to recover damages based upon Pinewave's installation of the windows. (Ex. K to the declaration of Dean Pappas-Admissions 1 and 2; First Amended Complaint par. 20). Plaintiff already recovered damages against Pinewave based on its poor workmanship in case CPF 05-505736. Since Pinewave cannot be jointly and severally liable to plaintiff in this case, International Window Corporation's cross-complaint for equitable indemnity fails as a matter of law. See Allis-Chalmers Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 1155. The court overrules all of Zurich American Insurance Company's objections to Everest Indemnity Insurance Company's evidence. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.
